entities.name: BORDETELLA PETRII
entities.description: A bacterial species identified through taxonomic and phylogenetic analysis

entities.name: BMCSI 3
entities.description: A specific strain of Bordetella petrii that is multi-metal resistant but multi-drug susceptible

entities.name: ANTIBIOTICS
entities.description: Chemical substances used to treat bacterial infections, to which BMCSI 3 is sensitive

entities.name: HEAVY METALS
entities.description: Toxic metallic elements to which BMCSI 3 exhibits resistance

entities.name: CADMIUM
entities.description: A heavy metal to which BMCSI 3 is resistant

entities.name: CHROMIUM
entities.description: A heavy metal to which BMCSI 3 is resistant

entities.name: COPPER
entities.description: A heavy metal to which BMCSI 3 is resistant

entities.name: MERCURY
entities.description: A heavy metal to which BMCSI 3 is resistant

entities.name: ARSENIC
entities.description: A metalloid/heavy metal to which BMCSI 3 is resistant

entities.name: MOTILITY
entities.description: The ability of the BMCSI 3 strain to move

entities.name: SPORE-FORMING
entities.description: The potential ability of the BMCSI 3 strain to form spores

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME SHOTGUN ASSEMBLY
entities.description: A sequencing method used to produce the 4.95 Mb genome of BMCSI 3

entities.name: TAXONOMIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis used to identify the strain as Bordetella petrii

entities.name: PHYLOGENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis used to identify the strain as Bordetella petrii

entities.name: COMPARATIVE GENOMIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis comparing BMCSI 3 with other free-living B. petrii genomes

entities.name: GENOMIC ISLANDS
entities.description: Large genomic regions carrying mobile genetic elements and resistance genes

entities.name: MOBILE GENETIC ELEMENTS
entities.description: DNA sequences that can move within or between genomes

entities.name: HEAVY METAL RESISTANT GENES
entities.description: Genes that provide resistance to heavy metals, located on genomic islands

entities.name: RESPONSE REGULATORS
entities.description: Genes coding for proteins that regulate cellular responses, located on genomic islands

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS
entities.description: Genes coding for proteins that control gene transcription, located on genomic islands

entities.name: TRANSPORTERS
entities.description: Genes coding for proteins that move substances across membranes, located on genomic islands

entities.name: MULTI-DRUG EFFLUX PUMPS
entities.description: Genes coding for proteins that pump drugs out of the cell, located on genomic islands

entities.name: TRANSPOSABLE ELEMENTS
entities.description: DNA sequences that can change position within the genome

entities.name: STRESS RESPONSE GENES
entities.description: Genes involved in the organism's response to environmental stress

entities.name: METABOLISM GENES
entities.description: Genes involved in the metabolic processes of the organism

entities.name: GENOMIC REORGANIZATION
entities.description: The process of structural changes in the genome leading to evolution

entities.name: PLASTICITY
entities.description: The ability of the genome to adapt and change

entities.name: HOSPITAL FACILITIES
entities.description: The source of antibiotic and heavy metal contamination in the soil

entities.name: HOSPITAL EFFLUENT
entities.description: The specific area surrounding which soil samples were collected
